skill.name: working-on-ancplua-plugins
skill.description: Instruction manual for creating, validating, publishing, and debugging plugins in the ANcpLua Claude plugin monorepo.
skill.summary: Guides agents through ANcpLua plugin structure, validation, publishing, and debugging workflows.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are markdown documentation examples rather than executable code. The remaining risk is that the skill asks agents to run local validation, permission, search, and log-inspection commands, so use should stay within a trusted repository.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Run Shell Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill contains shell command examples for validation, marketplace sync, JSON checks, path searches, and permission changes. These commands are legitimate repository workflows, but an agent could affect local files if used outside a trusted checkout.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are clear and intentional, but they are documentation rather than auto-executed code. Risk depends on whether an agent runs them without checking repository context.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem and Log Inspection Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill describes plugin directories, hidden configuration directories, executable script permissions, and Claude Code log locations. This is normal debugging guidance, but logs can contain sensitive local context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem references are present and relevant to local debugging. I found no evidence that the skill exfiltrates or automatically reads these files.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs Are Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to official Claude Code documentation, Conventional Commits documentation, and the source repository. They are references and manifest examples, not network calls or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain markdown links or repository metadata examples only. There is no code path that performs a network request.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography detections map to markdown filenames, table rows, version examples, or skill references. No hashing API, cipher selection, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static hits are explained by documentation text and markdown tokens. I found no executable crypto usage or security-sensitive algorithm choice.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Matches Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Most external-command detections are caused by inline code ticks and fenced examples in markdown. The package does not include Ruby files or executable scripts that use shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are markdown documentation, and the cited tokens are inline formatting or command names. No Ruby execution context exists in the reviewed file list.

dangerous_patterns.title: Manual Local Command Execution Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The only confirmed risky pattern is documentation that may cause an agent to run local shell commands. This is expected for a plugin development guide but requires trusted workspace context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and useful, but no file executes them automatically. The risk is operational rather than malicious.

content.value_statement: Plugin contributors need consistent structure, validation, and release steps. This skill gives agents repository-specific guidance for safe plugin changes.
actual_capabilities: Explains the ANcpLua plugin directory layout and required manifest locations.
Shows required plugin metadata fields and marketplace entry expectations.
Lists validation commands for full checks, marketplace sync, and single-plugin validation.
Provides debugging checks for JSON syntax, permissions, hardcoded paths, and Claude Code logs.
Points contributors to official Claude Code plugin, skill, hook, and marketplace documentation.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run validators by itself.
It is specific to the ANcpLua Claude plugin monorepo layout.
Its command examples require the repository tooling to exist locally.
It does not replace current upstream Claude Code documentation.

use_cases.title: Scaffold a New Plugin
use_cases.description: Follow the expected folders, manifest fields, README requirements, and validation checklist for a new repository plugin.

use_cases.title: Debug a Broken Plugin
use_cases.description: Check JSON syntax, permissions, hardcoded paths, component behavior, and local Claude Code logs.

use_cases.title: Review a Marketplace Change
use_cases.description: Verify version alignment, changelog updates, marketplace sync, and repository conventions before a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Plugin
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline the files and validation steps for a new ANcpLua plugin named <plugin-name>.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my changes in plugins/<plugin-name> against repository conventions and required validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Loading
prompt_templates.scenario: Maintainer investigating a plugin that does not appear in Claude Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to diagnose why plugins/<plugin-name> is not loading. Focus on manifests, paths, permissions, and logs.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Release Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release or pull request review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare a release review for plugins/<plugin-name>, including marketplace sync, changelog checks, and remaining risks.

output_examples.input: I want to add a new plugin called docs-helper.
output: A folder checklist for the plugin root and manifest directory.
Required metadata fields for the plugin manifest.
Validation steps to run before opening a pull request.

output_examples.input: My plugin does not load after restart.
output: A focused debugging sequence for manifest syntax, paths, permissions, and logs.
Likely causes ranked by repository convention violations.
A short list of files to inspect next.

output_examples.input: Review this plugin for marketplace readiness.
output: A readiness summary covering version alignment, README status, changelog status, and validation results.
Blocking issues separated from cleanup recommendations.

best_practices: Use the repository validation commands after every manifest or marketplace change.
Keep plugin assets at the plugin root unless the manifest specifically belongs in the manifest directory.
Replace local absolute paths with portable plugin-root variables before release.
anti_patterns: Do not place skills, commands, or hooks inside the manifest directory.
Do not hardcode personal filesystem paths in plugin configuration.
Do not update marketplace metadata without matching plugin version changes.

faq.question: Does this skill create plugins automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides repository instructions and command examples for an agent or contributor to follow.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only, but it includes local shell commands and log inspection guidance.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The URLs are documentation links and repository metadata examples.

faq.question: Can it validate a plugin by itself?
faq.answer: No. It tells users which repository validation commands to run.

faq.question: Is it useful outside the ANcpLua monorepo?
faq.answer: Only partially. Its paths, scripts, and conventions are designed for that specific repository.
